matt.lifts.heavy.summary: Matt is a 28-year-old Ohio lifter stuck on a 5-month strength plateau who is explicitly asking about 1:1 coaching structure and accountability.
matt.lifts.heavy.recommendedAction: Reply with the $200/mo coaching structure — monthly programming, form checks, weekly check-ins — and ask what his current split looks like.
matt.lifts.heavy.scoringReasoning: Explicit coaching ask in the DM, documented plateau frustration across multiple posts, genuine consistent lifting content with no spam signals. Coaching is Apex Fuel's top priority and Matt fits the ideal customer profile exactly.

suggestedReplies.label: Direct close
suggestedReplies.text: hey matt — 5 months stuck usually means something structural is off, not a motivation issue. my coaching is $200/mo: custom monthly programming, weekly form check videos, and i'm in your corner every day. want me to send the full breakdown?

suggestedReplies.label: Empathetic opener
suggestedReplies.text: that plateau feeling is genuinely rough, especially when you've done everything right. what does your current split look like? i have a hunch i know where the issue is.

suggestedReplies.label: Soft qualifier
suggestedReplies.text: hey! sounds like you're at the point where a coach would actually move the needle for you. i have a couple spots open — what's your training background look like?

enrichmentInfo.nicheEvidence: Posts squat PRs, meal preps, gym check-ins consistently across all 4 posts
enrichmentInfo.painPointDetail: stuck at the same bench and squat for 5 months, nothing moving the needle
enrichmentInfo.postContentSummary: Regular gym content — squat PRs, meal prep, training check-ins, one rest day post

matt.lifts.heavy.dm: hey so i've been stuck at the same bench and squat for like 5 months now and nothing i try is moving the needle. i've watched probably every youtube video at this point lol. i'm at the place where i think i actually need someone to tell me what i'm doing wrong and hold me accountable. what does your coaching actually look like month to month? like how involved are you with programming and checking form?

lifts.with.lena.summary: Lena is a competitive powerlifter with 61k followers pitching a meet-prep collab series with product gifting and rev share.
lifts.with.lena.recommendedAction: Evaluate her audience alignment first — if engagement holds (it does at ~5.5%), a product gifting + rev share collab during her meet prep series could drive real product sales.
lifts.with.lena.scoringReasoning: Genuine fitness creator with strong engagement for her tier, verified, and her audience is exactly Apex Fuel's target. The pitch is professional and specific. Worth a collab conversation, though coaching isn't the angle here.

suggestedReplies.label: Direct close
suggestedReplies.text: hey lena — your numbers look great and meet prep content is exactly the kind of thing our audience responds to. i'm open to product gifting + rev share. can you send me your media kit or rough rate card?

suggestedReplies.label: Empathetic opener
suggestedReplies.text: love the meet prep content — the consistency is real. we've done a few collab cycles before and they work best when the creator actually uses the product. what supplements are you currently running?

suggestedReplies.label: Soft qualifier
suggestedReplies.text: this could be a good fit timing-wise. what does the rev share structure look like on your end? and how many posts/stories are you thinking for the series?

enrichmentInfo.nicheEvidence: Every post is lifting-related — meet prep, PRs, training logs. No lifestyle fluff.
enrichmentInfo.postContentSummary: 100% powerlifting content — meet prep vlogs, squat PRs, programming breakdowns

grow_your_brand_fast.summary: Mass-sent spam DM from a social media growth service with no fitness relevance.
grow_your_brand_fast.scoringReasoning: Generic compliment followed by a pitch for follower growth services. No fitness content on the account, templated DM structure, and 'love your page' opener is a textbook spam signal. Zero fit with Apex Fuel.

enrichmentInfo.nicheEvidence: No fitness content whatsoever — account posts generic business tips and growth hacks

dan.the.desk.jockey.summary: Dan is a 35-year-old office worker who's been lifting casually for a year and is asking about pre-workout recommendations after losing energy mid-session.
dan.the.desk.jockey.recommendedAction: Recommend the pre-workout ($45) and explain the non-crash formula — his energy problem is exactly what it solves. Mention coaching as a follow-up if he wants to optimize more.
dan.the.desk.jockey.scoringReasoning: Real product inquiry with a specific use case. Account shows genuine lifting content even if sparse. The energy/fatigue angle is a direct fit for the pre-workout product. Mid-tier lead worth a prompt reply.

suggestedReplies.label: Direct close
suggestedReplies.text: hey dan — that mid-session crash is exactly what our pre-workout is built for. it's $45 and uses a non-crash formula so you don't die at hour 2. want me to send the link?

suggestedReplies.label: Empathetic opener
suggestedReplies.text: desk job + gym is genuinely a hard combo — by the time you get there your tank is empty. what time are you usually training? that might affect what i recommend.

suggestedReplies.label: Soft qualifier
suggestedReplies.text: good question — a few things could cause that. are you training after work or in the morning? and have you tried pre-workout before or would this be your first?
